pi_items.source: Web search confirmed this is the standard balanced equation for complete combustion of methane. Multiple authoritative chemistry sources (Brainly, PSU, Chemical Equation Balancer, WebQC) confirm this exact stoichiometry.
Specified in task.json and verified through stoichiometric calculations
Direct verification: 1.0 kg is the defined basis for all calculations

Stoichiometric calculation: Molar mass O2=32 g/mol (verified via web search). For 1 kg CH4 (molar mass=16 g/mol): moles CH4 = 1000g / 16 g/mol = 62.5 mol. From reaction stoichiometry (1:2 ratio), O2 needed = 62.5 mol × 2 = 125 mol. Mass = 125 mol × 32 g/mol = 4000 g = 4.0 kg

Stoichiometric calculation: Molar mass CO2=44 g/mol (verified via web search). From reaction stoichiometry (1:1 ratio with CH4), CO2 produced = 62.5 mol × 1 = 62.5 mol. Mass = 62.5 mol × 44 g/mol = 2750 g = 2.75 kg

Stoichiometric calculation: Molar mass H2O=18 g/mol (verified via web search). From reaction stoichiometry (2:1 ratio with CH4), H2O produced = 62.5 mol × 2 = 125 mol. Mass = 125 mol × 18 g/mol = 2250 g = 2.25 kg
Verification shows perfect mass balance: inputs (5.0 kg) = outputs (5.0 kg). Tolerance of ±0.1 kg is appropriate for floating-point calculations in software and accounts for rounding errors.
Standard atomic mass from periodic table and confirmed via web search of molar mass calculations
Standard atomic mass from periodic table and confirmed via web search of molar mass calculations
Standard atomic mass from periodic table and confirmed via web search of molar mass calculations
Calculated from atomic masses and confirmed via web search showing CH4 has molar mass of 16.04 g/mol (task uses rounded value of 16)
Calculated from atomic masses (2 × 16) and confirmed via multiple web search results showing O2 = 32.00 g/mol
Calculated from atomic masses (12 + 2×16) and confirmed via web search showing CO2 = 44.0 g/mol
Calculated from atomic masses (2×1 + 16) and confirmed via web search showing H2O = 18.0 g/mol

privileged_info_summary: The chemical reaction stoichiometry for methane combustion is verified as CH4 + 2O2 → CO2 + 2H2O. All molar masses are correct: CH4 (16 g/mol), O2 (32 g/mol), CO2 (44 g/mol), H2O (18 g/mol). For 1 kg of methane input, the stoichiometrically calculated outputs are: oxygen input 4.0 kg, CO2 output 2.75 kg, water output 2.25 kg. Mass balance is perfect: 5.0 kg input equals 5.0 kg output, well within the 0.1 kg tolerance.
